title: Loading State
description: Loading State communicates ongoing work during asynchronous operations. It supports inline loading, richer messaging, overlay modes, fullscreen blocking states, and projected custom content.
intro: Use `ui-loading-state` when users need feedback that work is in progress. Choose the lightest pattern that still explains the delay: inline when content is not yet present, overlay when existing content is being refreshed, and fullscreen only for blocking transitions.

featureSections.title: Basic inline loading
featureSections.description: The simplest pattern is an inline loading state used before content exists or when a small section waits on data.

featureSections.title: Loading messages and scale
featureSections.description: Titles, descriptions, and size changes help match the tone of short refreshes versus more substantial waits.

featureSections.title: Custom projected progress content
featureSections.description: Projected content is useful for richer setup flows or multi-step initialization where a plain spinner is not enough.

featureSections.title: Overlay on existing card content
featureSections.description: Overlay mode works well when content already exists but is temporarily refreshing. Blur helps shift attention to the loading message.

featureSections.title: Overlay on dense lists or records
featureSections.description: For tables and lists, a lighter overlay can preserve context while still communicating that the section is updating.

featureSections.title: Fullscreen blocking transitions
featureSections.description: Fullscreen loading should be reserved for app-level or route-level transitions where the whole interface is temporarily unavailable.

featureSections.title: Embedded panel or dashboard layout
featureSections.description: In real products, loading states usually sit inside richer shells with search, messages, and content blocks rather than on a blank surface.

accessibility.title: Status communication
accessibility.body: A loading state should clearly communicate that work is in progress and, when possible, what is being loaded or updated. Short messages are usually better than generic filler.

accessibility.title: Overlay behavior
accessibility.body:
When overlay mode is used, ensure the surrounding experience still makes sense for keyboard and screen-reader users.

| Mode | Guidance |
| --- | --- |
| Inline loading | Use when content has not appeared yet. |
| Container overlay | Use when existing content is refreshing and context should remain visible. |
| Fullscreen overlay | Reserve for blocking transitions that truly affect the whole page. |

accessibility.title: Custom projected content
accessibility.body: Projected content should add useful status context such as setup steps or progress, not duplicate the same message with more noise.

apiSections.title: LoadingStateComponent - ui-loading-state
apiSections.lead: Standalone loading feedback component for inline, overlay, and fullscreen loading scenarios. It supports messaging, spinner scaling, optional blur, and projected custom content.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Overall scale of the loading state.
Expands overlay mode to the full viewport.
Controls whether a non-fullscreen overlay is currently shown.

blocks.title: Projected content
rows: Optional projected content rendered under the main loading message.

paragraphs: Choose the lightest possible loading pattern. Inline and container overlays are usually preferable to fullscreen loading.
If the wait is frequent or long, refine the loading copy and surrounding experience rather than relying on a generic spinner alone.
blocks.title: Notes
